title: Project cannot install on supported Node 24 because @types/node is pinned to Node 20 types
reasoning: The package declares Node 24 as a supported runtime, but the TypeScript ambient Node API package is constrained to Node 20. That can make typechecking diverge from the advertised runtime surface: Node 22/24 globals and API signatures may be missing or typed differently, while APIs removed or changed after Node 20 may still appear valid. This is a release/config hazard because contributors and CI can get misleading type results for a runtime the package explicitly supports.
recommendation: Align @types/node with the supported runtime range, for example by using a Node 22-compatible type package if CI standardizes on Node 22, or by documenting and testing separate typecheck jobs for each supported major.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No typecheck command or CI matrix is included in the reviewed evidence, and the test script only runs selected test files rather than validating runtime type declarations across supported Node majors.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a typecheck script and run it under the supported Node major used for builds, or add CI jobs for Node 22 and Node 24 if both are intentionally supported.
minimumFixScope: Update package.json devDependency/runtime support policy and add or document the matching typecheck coverage.
